use axum::{Router, routing::{get, post}};

async fn login_handler() -> &'static str { "ok" }
async fn logout_handler() -> &'static str { "bye" }
async fn root_handler() -> &'static str { "/" }

fn build_routes() -> Router {
    Router::new()
        .route("/login", post(login_handler))
        .route("/logout", get(logout_handler))
}

// Real-world canonical axum pattern: async fn main() with no return type.
// Pinned regression: rust/queries.scm must extract this as Function so
// the framework_ref's enclosing-fn lookup succeeds.
#[tokio::main]
async fn main() {
    let app = Router::new().route("/", get(root_handler));
    let listener = tokio::net::TcpListener::bind("127.0.0.1:3000").await.unwrap();
    axum::serve(listener, app).await.unwrap();
}
